---
title: "data_wrangle"
format: html
---

# Libraries
```{r, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
```

# Load in CSV From Data Exploration
```{r, message=FALSE}
events <- read_csv(here::here('data','wwe_events.csv'))
match_rating <- read_csv(here::here("data","wwe_match_rating.csv"))

```

# Every Superstar
```{r}
superstars <- match_rating %>%
  select(Index, Opponent.1, Opponent.2) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Opponent.1, Opponent.2),
    names_to = "Side",
    values_to = "Team"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Team = str_replace_all(Team, ",\\s*$", "")) %>%  # remove trailing commas
  separate_rows(Team, sep = "&|,") %>%                    # split on & and ,
  mutate(SuperstarName = str_trim(Team)) %>%              # clean whitespace
  filter(SuperstarName != "") %>%                         # drop empty rows
  select(MatchIndex = Index, SuperstarName)

superstars
```

# Table for teams
```{r}
teams <- match_rating %>%
  select(Index, Opponent.1, Opponent.2) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Opponent.1, Opponent.2),
    names_to = "Side",
    values_to = "Team"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Team = str_trim(str_replace_all(Team, ",\\s*$", ""))) %>%
  filter(str_detect(Team, "&")) %>%        # keep only entries with & (tag teams)
  select(MatchIndex = Index, Team)

teams
```


# Saves to a folder named 'output' in your current working directory
```{r}

# Superstar
write.csv(superstars, file = "output/superstars.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Teams
write.csv(teams, file = "output/teams.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Add Match Rating csv into output
write.csv(match_rating, file = "output/match_rating.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Add Events csv into output
write.csv(events, file = "output/events.csv", row.names = FALSE)



```
